name: Casi-Style Chili
recipeIngredient: 4 Jalapeño chiles (with stems and deveined), halved
2 lb Beef chuck, cut into 1-1/2 inch cubes
2 tbsp Kidney suet or vegetable oil
1/4 cup Masa harina (finely ground maize flour)
recipeInstructions:
1. Brown the beef cubes in a bit of oil or fat.
2. Add chopped onions and cook until softened.
3. Stir in tomato sauce, beer, beef stock, halved Jalapeño chiles, cumin, garlic powder, black pepper, and 2 tablespoons of chili powder. Simmer on low heat for 2 hours, stirring occasionally, until the meat is tender.
4. Quickly stir a thin paste made from masa harina and water into the chili to thicken it. If lumps form, stir quickly.
5. Add the remaining chili powder and paprika. Simmer an additional 15 minutes.
6. Remove the Jalapeño chiles before serving.
[CASI (Chili Appreciation Society International) chili-cookoff winners often use blended chili powder and remove the Jalapeño chiles before serving. They also add masa, a flour made from dried maize available in Latin markets, to thicken the chili.]
